summary: skills-detector ships an install-time host-recon beacon and reverse shell
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js automatically (1) executes `whoami` and `id`, collects `os.hostname()`, `os.platform()`, cwd, and CI-related env vars (CI, GITHUB_REPOSITORY, NODE_ENV), and exfiltrates them via HTTPS GET to the out-of-band domain `wrr93eupmqa7vehh0twtomxlscy9m5au.oastify.com` (Burp Collaborator/OAST infrastructure used as an exfil callback); and (2) opens a TCP socket to the hardcoded address `10.10.10.247:4444` and pipes `/bin/sh` (or `cmd.exe` on Windows) stdio across it, yielding an interactive reverse shell on the installer's machine. Both behaviors fire unconditionally from a lifecycle hook, with no opt-in or feature gate.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
